title: Iron Snake Mode: Animated Level-to-Level Board Morph Transition (JOE-5)
content:
## Overview

When a player completes a level in Iron Snake mode, the board does not hard-cut to the new shape. Instead, the playable region **morphs cell-by-cell** from the old shape into the new one, driven at the game's own move cadence. The snake never moves or teleports during the transition; the new board is grown around the snake's current position.

## Key Lever: Single Board Mask

All three board-drawing functions (`drawGroundPlane`, `drawGridLines`, `drawGroundBorder`) read a single field — `boardMask: Uint8Array | null` (`src/main.ts`) — to determine which cells are playable. Assigning an interpolated display mask to `this.boardMask` each frame causes the floor tiles, grid lines, and border outline to all reshape for free, with no changes to any rendering code.

## Board Generation: Growing Around the Snake

The new board is generated by `growIronSnakeBoard` (`src/iron-snake.ts`), which:

1. Seeds a clear room around the combined bounding box of the snake's current cells.
2. Grows fresh overlapping rooms outward until the board reaches target fill.

This guarantees a visually different shape every level regardless of whether the grid size increases, because the surrounding region is always regenerated from scratch rather than embedded from the old board. The snake's grid coordinates are preserved across the level boundary; if the grid grows, `translateSnakes` shifts both snakes by the centering offset so their screen position stays constant.

The previous approach (`reseatKeepingShape`) generated a random board first and then relocated the snake to a preferred anchor, causing a visible teleport. The entire shape-rotation reseat chain — `placeRigid`, `layoutBody`, `placeSnakeKeepingShape`, `rotateOffset`, `rotateDirection`, `reseatKeepingShape` — has been deleted as dead code. `vectorToDirection` was retained as it is still used by tail-direction rendering logic.

## Morph Animation Mechanics

The morph is driven by a `requestAnimationFrame` loop (modeled on the existing `autoRotateStep` pattern) while the game logic interval is paused.

**Mask construction at morph start:**
- `startDisplay` — the old board's playable cells stamped into the new grid dimensions, center-aligned, with every current snake segment and food cell unioned in so nothing floats over void.
- `targetDisplay` — a copy of the new `boardMask` produced by `growIronSnakeBoard`.
- `changed` — the list of cells where `startDisplay ≠ targetDisplay`, **sorted by ascending distance from the board center**, producing an inside-out wave: the arena appears to grow outward and old-only cells peel away as the wave passes.

**Each step:** a batch of cells (`LEVEL_MORPH_CELLS_PER_STEP`) is revealed from the `changed` list, the working buffer is assigned to `this.boardMask`, and `draw()` is called. Each step is held for approximately one snake-move duration (bounded by `LEVEL_MORPH_MIN_STEP_MS`), so the reshape unfolds at the game's own rhythm rather than a fixed wall-clock duration.

**On completion:** `this.boardMask` is restored to the exact `targetReal` value (preserving `null` for full-rectangle boards), `draw()` is called once cleanly, the logic interval is restarted at the new `gameSpeed`, and transition flags are cleared.

## Tuning Constants (`src/main.ts`)

| Constant | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `LEVEL_MORPH_MIN_STEPS` | Minimum number of morph steps |
| `LEVEL_MORPH_MAX_STEPS` | Maximum number of morph steps |
| `LEVEL_MORPH_CELLS_PER_STEP` | Cells revealed per step |
| `LEVEL_MORPH_MIN_STEP_MS` | Minimum wall-clock duration per step |

## New Methods and Fields (`src/main.ts`)

- `startLevelMorph` — builds the start/target display masks and `changed` list, tears down the logic interval, sets `isTransitioning = true`, and starts the RAF loop.
- `morphStep` — the per-frame RAF callback; reveals cells incrementally, calls `draw()`, and calls `finishLevelMorph` when all cells are revealed.
- `finishLevelMorph` — restores `targetReal`, restarts the logic interval, clears flags.
- `cancelLevelMorph` — cancels any in-flight RAF and clears `isTransitioning`; called from `startNewGame` and both back-to-menu handlers.
- `applyLevelAdvance` — replaces the old `reseatKeepingShape` pipeline; keeps snakes in place and calls `growIronSnakeBoard`.
- `isTransitioning: boolean` — suppresses normal per-tick repaints in `updateSinglePlayer` and `updateTwoPlayer` while a morph is in progress.
- `transitionRafId: number | null` — stores the active RAF handle for cancellation.

## Behavior Notes

- The morph **always plays**; the earlier `prefers-reduced-motion` gate that caused the transition to be invisible for users with that OS setting enabled has been removed.
- The logic interval is torn down at morph start and restarted in `finishLevelMorph`; guards in `updateSinglePlayer` and `updateTwoPlayer` suppress the normal per-tick repaint while transitioning.
- `gridSize` (and thus cell scale) jumps to the new value at morph frame 0. Per-level grid growth is typically small (0–1 cell), so the effect is subtle. Animating the scale is out of scope.
- Classic mode's `expandGrid()` is unaffected and retains its instant swap.
- Iron Snake two-player and bot-vs-bot are covered: the logic loop is paused during the morph, so neither snake can die mid-transition.

## Files

- `src/iron-snake.ts` — `growIronSnakeBoard`
- `src/main.ts` — all morph orchestration, new fields/constants, `applyLevelAdvance`, deleted reseat chain
